name: Fonfría desde As Nogais — circular por el Torre de Doncos
region_intro: Mountain biking in Cruzul-Agüeira offers diverse landscapes within the Special Conservation Area, characterized by a blend of Atlantic and Mediterranean ecosystems. The terrain features impressive karst formations like Os Grobos Forest, ancient oak and chestnut groves, and mountainous areas with significant elevation changes. Riders can expect varied conditions, from river basins to higher elevations, providing a rich environment for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails in Cruzul-Agüeira The most popular mountain bike trail is Hiking from Teso da Lama Figueiro y Becerreá to As Nogais - circular route through the Special Conservation Area Cruzul-Agüeira, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the protected conservation area, offering challenging climbs and natural beauty. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mosteiro de Santa María de Penamaior from Becerreá — circular, a difficult 18.2 miles (29.4 km) path. This route explores the area around Becerreá, featuring significant elevation gain and scenic views. Local mountain bikers also love the As Nogais - circular route along the Navia River - Cruzul-Agüeira Ecological Conservation Area, a 8.9 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through the Navia River basin, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Mountain biking in Cruzul-Agüeira is defined by dramatic karst landscapes, ancient mixed forests, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Cruzul-Agüeira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from around 5 reviews. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cruzul-Agüeira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain biking terrain can I expect in Cruzul-Agüeira?
A: Cruzul-Agüeira offers a diverse range of terrain, blending Atlantic and Mediterranean ecosystems. You'll encounter impressive karst formations, such as those in Os Grobos Forest, ancient oak and chestnut groves, and mountainous areas with significant elevation changes. Trails can vary from river basins to higher elevations, providing a rich and varied environment for mountain biking.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cruzul-Agüeira?
A: While Cruzul-Agüeira is known for its challenging routes, there are options for various skill levels. The region offers paths suitable for beginners, corresponding to STS S0 - S1 difficulty, typically with up to 1,000 feet of elevation gain. These trails allow riders to enjoy the natural beauty without extreme technical demands.

Q: What are the options for advanced mountain bikers in Cruzul-Agüeira?
A: Advanced riders will find plenty of challenging routes in Cruzul-Agüeira. Many trails feature significant elevation changes, with climbs of up to 3,000 feet or more, and correspond to STS S2 and S3-S6 difficulty levels. These routes require very good fitness and advanced riding skills, with some sections potentially requiring you to push your bike.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Cruzul-Agüeira pass through stunning natural and historical sites. You can explore the 'magical and secret' Os Grobos Forest with its unique limestone formations and ancient trees, or ride through the Mediterranean oak grove in Cruzul and the chestnut grove in Agüeira. Historical elements like the 12th-century Church of San Xoán and the Albergue A Reboleira, an old pilgrim hostel, also dot the landscape.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cruzul-Agüeira?
A: Yes, many routes in Cruzul-Agüeira are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211375416/as-nogais-circular-route-along-the-navia-river-cruzul-agueeira-ecological-conservation-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais - circular route along the Navia River - Cruzul-Agüeira Ecological Conservation Area&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.9-mile (14.4 km) trail.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cruzul-Agüeira?
A: The region's ancient mixed forests, particularly the oak and chestnut groves, are especially vibrant in autumn, offering stunning scenery with contrasting deciduous and perennial species. While specific seasonal conditions can vary, spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful landscapes for mountain biking.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cruzul-Agüeira?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cruzul-Agüeira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Riders often praise the dramatic karst landscapes, the ancient mixed forests, and the diverse network of trails that cater to a wide range of abilities, all set within a pristine natural environment.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Cruzul-Agüeira?
A: There are 15 mountain bike tours available in Cruzul-Agüeira on komoot, offering a variety of experiences across the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking longer distances and significant challenges, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211406295/fonfria-desde-as-nogais-circular-por-el-torre-de-doncos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonfría desde As Nogais — circular por el Torre de Doncos&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial ride of over 29 miles (47 km) with considerable elevation gain, providing a full day's adventure.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that include historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210569136/mosteiro-de-santa-maria-de-penamaior-from-becerrea-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mosteiro de Santa María de Penamaior from Becerreá — circular&lt;/a&gt; route explores the area around Becerreá, potentially bringing you close to historical structures like the Monastery of Santa María de Penamaior, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211428925/hiking-from-teso-da-lama-figueiro-y-becerrea-to-as-nogais-circular-route-through-the-special-conservation-area-cruzul-agueeira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking from Teso da Lama Figueiro y Becerreá to As Nogais - circular route through the Special Conservation Area Cruzul-Agüeira&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.8-mile (36.7 km) trail that traverses the protected conservation area, featuring challenging climbs and demanding terrain.
tips: Nestled in the landscape of the Navia river valley, the medieval defensive watchtower, the Tower of Doncos is a unique historical complex.
Upper Fontfria, summit of the Becerreá mountain range near As Nogais. A good place to stop to rest, have a coffee and eat something before going down to the Navia river valley.
